Patch Set 1:

Abandoned for later build 65301

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

This looks correct, but I think there is another issue as well, which I alluded to in the original ticket (probably wasn't very clear).

The assert hit is at the top of ptlrpc_set_add_req:
        LASSERT(req->rq_import->imp_state != LUSTRE_IMP_IDLE);

And imp_inflight is incremented in:
                ptlrpc_send_new_req(req);

Which is called at the end of ptlrpc_set_add_req.

So using imp_inflight leaves a race, since we assert !IDLE before we increment it.

Would it be easier to just use the import refcount, like we do when deciding if a connection is ready to idle?  (Or we could just use the same check entirely, with an adjustment for having the extra ref for the disconnect?)

Patch Set 6:

in the end, the exact moment a connection goes idle isn't very important, IMO. it just need to be safe and disconnect and disconnect at some point.

Patch Set 6:

Yeah, I agree with that.  Just has to be safe.

I was only suggesting a possible imp_inflight timing change because of Andreas's comment about imp_refcount being tricky because of pinger stuff.  Cray and (I think) Fujitsu planned to use pinger off and do imperative eviction from outside Lustre; Cray never finished their work and dropped it, I think Fujitsu uses pinger off for real?

But if we can make imp_refcount checking safe then obvsly that's fine.

Patch Set 6:

in any case it's good to know about pinger, thanks.

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 6:

Nah, as someone adjacent to that failed effort, Cray made a lot of noise about planning to not use the pinger, didn't quite finish the work, then dropped it from the roadmap.  So they definitely still use the pinger.

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)
Abandoned

commit.subject: LU-12364 ptlrpc: disconnect shouldn't transit through IDLE state
commit.message:
LU-12364 ptlrpc: disconnect shouldn't transit through IDLE state

otherwise this leaves a window for another thread to find
the import idling which is not correct (see assertion in
ptlrpc_set_add_req().
also, check for import's reference to prevent a race
between RPC allocation and sending.

Change-Id: I159f6851a9d5191a5e6c26ff1773fbc3389cad70
Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
